locations.label: Barcelona
locations.note: Barcelona uses official Spanish Interior Ministry annual crime-balance releases for the municipality of Barcelona. The app combines the verified year-end municipal tables published by the ministry; rate-per-100k is not shown because this source path does not bundle a comparable municipal population series in the same extract.

sources.label: Spanish Interior Ministry balances and reports archive
Spanish Interior Ministry crime statistics portal publications

locations.label: Berlin
locations.note: Berlin uses official Polizei Berlin atlas categories. Some categories are umbrella groups while others are nested subtypes, so the stacks are best read as comparison views rather than deduplicated totals.

sources.label: Polizei Berlin Kriminalitätsatlas
Polizei Berlin PKS archive and historical atlas reports
Current district workbook from the official atlas

locations.label: Frankfurt
locations.note: Frankfurt currently uses official citywide open-data crime series from the City of Frankfurt and the Hessisches Landeskriminalamt. These categories are annual city totals rather than district-level atlas slices.

sources.label: City of Frankfurt open data: crimes by offense type
City of Frankfurt open data: citywide demographic indicators

locations.label: Hamburg
locations.note: Hamburg uses official Polizeiliche Kriminalstatistik yearbooks published by the Hamburg police. The app currently shows a stable citywide subset of annual recorded-crime counts from the official PKS comparison tables.

sources.label: Polizei Hamburg: Polizeiliche Kriminalstatistik service page
Polizei Hamburg PKS yearbook 2024

locations.label: London
locations.note: London uses official Metropolitan Police recorded-crime categories aggregated to calendar years. A few categories were renamed over time, and those official aliases are merged where the dataset clearly indicates the same series.

sources.label: MPS Recorded Crime: Geographic Breakdown dataset
ONS borough population estimates via London Datastore
ONS England and Wales local-authority population workbook

locations.label: Luton
locations.note: Luton uses official Community Safety Partnership and local-authority crime tables. The series is annual, based on rolling 12-month totals at the year ending each March, and it is citywide rather than neighbourhood-level.

sources.label: ONS recorded crime data at Community Safety Partnership and local authority level
ONS population estimates detailed time series

locations.label: Milan
locations.note: Milan uses official Comune di Milano open-data series for reported crimes and official resident-population series for the rate view. The figures are annual citywide totals, not district-level cuts.

sources.label: Comune di Milano open data: reported crimes 2004-2023
Comune di Milano open data: calculated resident population by sex 1936-2023

locations.label: Paris
locations.note: Paris uses the official communal crime base published by the French Interior Ministry and SSMSI. The figures are citywide commune totals rather than arrondissement-level series.

sources.label: French Interior Ministry / SSMSI communal crime base

locations.label: Rome
locations.note: Rome uses official Roma Capitale urban-security workbooks. These are annual citywide totals rather than neighborhood slices, with yearly offense-type tables and resident population used for the rate view.

sources.label: Roma Capitale statistics: legality and public safety

locations.label: Valencia
locations.note: Barcelona uses official Spanish Interior Ministry annual crime-balance releases for the municipality of Barcelona. The app combines the verified year-end municipal tables published by the ministry; rate-per-100k is not shown because this source path does not bundle a comparable municipal population series in the same extract.

sources.label: Spanish Interior Ministry balances and reports archive
Spanish Interior Ministry crime statistics portal publications
